steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must accomplish two things: (1) explain whom the A.M. Turing Award is named for, and (2) identify one recipient of it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The A.M. Turing Award is given for "major contributions of lasting importance to computing.": explains the award's purpose but names neither the namesake nor a recipient. ✗
  • Choice B — The A.M. Turing Award, which is named for British mathematician Alan Turing, was given to Judea Pearl in 2011.: names Alan Turing as the namesake and Judea Pearl as a recipient — both requirements met. ✓
  • Choice C — In 2011, Judea Pearl won the A.M. Turing Award, which is given for "major contributions of lasting importance to computing.": identifies a recipient but omits the namesake. ✗
  • Choice D — It was in 2011 that Judea Pearl won the A.M. Turing Award.: names only a recipient; no namesake information. ✗
B is correct.
